meaning: adj.不会受伤害的,无懈可击的
examples: Many daughters assume that their mothers are invulnerable.
He might have been called the invulnerable dwarf of the fray.
Micro-Mechanics plans to produce a chip that, because it uses only microscopic mechanical switches, will be invulnerable to radiation damage.
These 200 Best Small Companies in America ( the list) are far from invulnerable.
Last year's question of whether Google might be invulnerable to a recession has officially been answered.
